mcqs.question: What is the maximum throughput of Pure ALOHA?
mcqs.explanation: Pure ALOHA has a maximum throughput of 18.4% (S = G × e^(-2G) at G = 0.5), meaning only 18.4% of the time is used for successful transmission.

mcqs.question: What is the vulnerable time for Pure ALOHA?
mcqs.explanation: The vulnerable time in Pure ALOHA is 2T, where T is the frame transmission time. This is because a collision can occur if another frame is transmitted either T time before or T time after the current frame.

mcqs.question: Which protocol uses collision detection (CSMA/CD)?
mcqs.explanation: CSMA/CD (Collision Detection) is used in Ethernet (IEEE 802.3) networks. Stations monitor the channel while transmitting and stop immediately if a collision is detected.

mcqs.question: Which protocol uses collision avoidance (CSMA/CA)?
mcqs.explanation: CSMA/CA (Collision Avoidance) is used in WiFi (IEEE 802.11) wireless networks. It avoids collisions by using RTS/CTS handshaking and Inter-Frame Spaces (IFS) before transmitting.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum throughput of Slotted ALOHA?
mcqs.explanation: Slotted ALOHA has a maximum throughput of 36.8% (S = G × e^(-G) at G = 1), which is double that of Pure ALOHA because slots reduce the vulnerable time.

mcqs.question: What is the minimum Ethernet frame size required for CSMA/CD to work properly?
mcqs.explanation: The minimum Ethernet frame size is 64 bytes (512 bits). This ensures the transmission time is at least twice the maximum propagation delay, allowing collision detection.

mcqs.question: In p-persistent CSMA, when the channel is idle, what happens?

options.text: Device transmits with probability p and waits with probability (1-p)
Device waits for a random time

mcqs.explanation: In p-persistent CSMA, when the channel is idle, the device transmits with probability p. With probability (1-p), it waits until the next time slot to try again.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Jam signal in Ethernet?

options.text: To inform all stations about a collision

mcqs.explanation: The Jam signal is sent after detecting a collision to ensure all other stations become aware that a collision has occurred and stop transmitting, preventing further collisions.

mcqs.question: What does NAV (Network Allocation Vector) represent in WiFi?

options.text: The signal strength of the access point
The time duration for which the channel will be busy

mcqs.explanation: NAV is a virtual carrier sense timer that indicates how long the channel will be busy. Stations update their NAV based on duration fields in RTS/CTS and data frames.

mcqs.question: Which CSMA variant has the highest probability of collision?
mcqs.explanation: 1-persistent CSMA has the highest collision probability because when the channel becomes idle, multiple stations may try to transmit simultaneously with probability 1.

mcqs.question: What is the main reason CSMA/CD cannot be used in wireless networks?

options.text: Collision detection is difficult due to hidden node problem and signal attenuation
CSMA/CD is not standardized for wireless

mcqs.explanation: CSMA/CD cannot be used in wireless networks because stations may not be able to detect collisions due to the hidden node problem and signal propagation characteristics, making collision avoidance necessary.

mcqs.question: What is the function of RTS/CTS in 802.11?

options.text: To reserve the channel and solve hidden node problem

mcqs.explanation: RTS/CTS (Request to Send/Clear to Send) is a handshaking mechanism that reserves the channel before data transmission and solves the hidden node problem in wireless networks.

mcqs.question: At what offered load (G) does Slotted ALOHA achieve maximum throughput?
mcqs.explanation: Slotted ALOHA achieves maximum throughput of 36.8% when G = 1. This is derived by differentiating S = G × e^(-G) and setting it to zero.

mcqs.question: Which IFS (Inter-Frame Space) has the highest priority in 802.11?
mcqs.explanation: SIFS (Short IFS) has the highest priority and is used for immediate response frames like ACK, CTS, and data fragments. It is shorter than DIFS, PIFS, and EIFS.

mcqs.question: In a CSMA/CD network with propagation delay of 25.6 μs, what is the minimum frame transmission time required?
mcqs.explanation: For CSMA/CD to work, the minimum frame transmission time must be at least twice the propagation delay (2 × 25.6 μs = 51.2 μs) to ensure collision detection.
